title: The Most Powerful Engine on Earth

beats.narration: From two hundred miles up, it looks almost gentle. A slow white pinwheel, turning over the sea. It is the most powerful engine on Earth.
beats.understand: establish the storm as deceptively serene from orbit before the mechanism reveal

footage.intent: a broad hurricane spiral seen from the ISS, calm and slow-turning

beats.narration: It is born from a single thing. Warm ocean, above twenty-six degrees Celsius, breathing heat and water into the sky.
beats.understand: the one ingredient a hurricane needs — a warm sea surface

footage.intent: warm tropical ocean under broken cloud, distinct from the spiral

beats.narration: That warm, wet air rises. High up it cools and rains, and every drop that condenses releases heat, pulling more air upward behind it. The storm becomes its own engine, feeding on the sea.
beats.understand: the self-feeding heat-engine feedback loop — the mechanism at the heart of the film

footage.intent: the churning storm from orbit, cloud tops boiling upward — the engine at work

beats.narration: We measure them by their winds. A category one bends the trees at a hundred and nineteen. A three tears off roofs. A five, past two hundred and fifty, erases whole coastlines.
beats.understand: anchor the destructive scale by wind speed so the reveal lands

beats.narration: And at the very center, calm. A ring of towering cloud around an eye of perfect stillness, circled by winds strong enough to release the energy of ten thousand bombs a day.
beats.understand: the showpiece — the eye, the emotional and visual peak, carrying the power number

footage.intent: the clear eye of the hurricane from orbit, ringed by cloud — the money shot

beats.narration: Warm water and air. That is all it is. And once it begins to turn, nothing on Earth can stop it.
beats.understand: thesis — the storm is a simple machine of overwhelming power; a true bookend that returns to the exact opening frame (callback); the film ENDS here
beats.text: Nothing on Earth can stop it.

footage.intent: return to the calm Helene spiral of the opening — the thesis lands on the storm that opened the film (callback)
